import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from ta import momentum

def generate_signals(df, params):
    """
    RSI-based mean reversion strategy for EUR/USD.
    
    Rationale: RSI extremes (overbought/oversold) tend to mean-revert
    due to behavioral overreaction and technical levels acting as support/resistance.
    
    Args:
        df: DataFrame with OHLC columns
        params: dict with 'lookback', 'rsi_low', 'rsi_high'
    
    Returns:
        pd.Series of signals: 1 (long), -1 (short), 0 (neutral)
    """
    lookback = params.get('lookback', 14)
    rsi_low = params.get('rsi_low', 30)
    rsi_high = params.get('rsi_high', 70)
    
    # Calculate RSI
    rsi = momentum.rsi(df['close'], window=lookback)
    
    # Generate signals
    signal = pd.Series(0, index=df.index, dtype=int)
    signal[rsi < rsi_low] = 1    # Go long when oversold (RSI < low threshold)
    signal[rsi > rsi_high] = -1  # Go short when overbought (RSI > high threshold)
    
    return signal
rationale: RSI extremes revert to mean due to behavioral overreaction and technical support/resistance.
